classification.analysis: Starmer faces existential crisis after May 7 local elections saw Labour lose 1,500 council seats to Reform UK's 1,454 gains. Four ministers resigned by May 12-13, with 92 MPs demanding his exit. Eurasia Group gives 80% probability of 2026 ouster. Reform leads polls by 9+ points. Health Secretary Streeting positioning for leadership challenge. Starmer now least popular PM in UK polling history since 1978.

Merz's grand coalition has collapsed in polls one year after taking office, falling below majority. AfD now leads nationally at 27%, up from 24% in February. Only 13% satisfied with government; chancellor's approval at catastrophic 16%. Coalition infighting over immigration and economy persists. Critical state elections loom where AfD could win governments. Brandmauer firewall intact but coalition visibly failing.

Macron, after nine years, is a lame-duck president with catastrophic 18% approval. Far-right National Rally leads presidential polls at 35-36% with Jordan Bardella, while the republican front collapses—only 46% would block RN in a runoff. Over 300,000 protested on May Day against his labor policies. With Macron term-limited and his centrist coalition polling at just 13-14%, the far-right's path to power in 2027 appears open.

Sánchez governs with minority after Junts withdrawal but survives via opposition divisions. His defiant stance against Trump over Iran war bases proved popular domestically and helped PSOE resist collapse in March Castile-León election. However coalition trails PP by 2-3 points, lost Aragón badly in February, and faces Andalusia vote this weekend where PSOE expects historic losses. Wife formally charged with corruption April 13th deepens personal vulnerability.

Frederiksen's centrist coalition suffered historic losses in March, with Social Democrats posting worst result since 1903. Coalition collapsed; Venstre leader Poulsen became formateur May 11 after Frederiksen failed to form government. Danish People's Party tripled support to 9.1% at election, now polls at 10.3%, becoming largest blue-bloc party. Seven years in power, addressing immigration rightward but bleeding voters left and right, coalition now caretaker status.

Albanese won a historic landslide in May 2025, but One Nation has surged to overtake the Coalition, winning the Farrer by-election in May 2026. Post-budget polling showed One Nation ahead of Labor on primary votes (32% vs 28.5%), with the two-party result 51-49 'too close to call'. Albanese's approval collapsed to 40-59%, his worst since taking office. Labor still leads the Coalition 52.5-47.5 on two-party preferred, but the far-right is now the primary opposition.

Martin's centrist coalition has dropped from 37% to 33.7% in three months, losing its projected majority. Fianna Fáil faces catastrophic polling in this week's by-elections, with just 4% support in Dublin Central. Internal party pressure mounts ahead of the centenary Ard Fheis, with speculation Martin may soon face leadership challenges despite his public defiance.

Montenegro's minority government has declined from 32% at election to 23.3%, now trailing PS at 28.4%. Chega holds steady at 22.9% and exercises de facto influence, having supported government immigration restrictions. Recent police reinforcement announcement and CDS-PP leadership contest reflect governance strain. Storm damage and Iran war economic impact compound pressures. Cordon sanitaire breached in practice without formal coalition.

Støre's Labour won re-election in September 2025 with a dramatic comeback, but has since collapsed from 28% to 21.9% while Progress Party surged to 29.1%, now leading polls. In early May, FrP leader Listhaug accused Labour of running a 'troll factory,' deploying aggressive populist rhetoric. With 3+ years until 2029 election, Støre faces a far-right ascendant and his minority government increasingly embattled.

Japan's first female PM, winning a historic LDP landslide in February 2026. Approval remains high, but Takaichi's hard-line conservative stance and absorption of Sanseito-style rhetoric on immigration and nationalism mark a clear drift toward the Phase II boundary. Mainstream right shifting right is the classic mechanism by which the doom loop progresses without an obvious far-right electoral breakthrough.

Michal's Reform-Eesti 200 coalition polls at catastrophic 14%, down from early-year levels, securing just 12.9% of projected seats. After expelling SDE in March 2025, the government has lost viability; opposition commands 73% support. Recent news focuses on deficit-reduction pledges and presidential maneuvering. EKRE stable at 12.6% but overshadowed by Isamaa's 26% surge. Macro weakness and coalition exhaustion define the moment.

Kristersson's government is in late Phase II collapse. The far-right Sweden Democrats now poll higher than his Moderates, the Liberals dropped red lines against SD in March, and the citizenship law vote chaos triggered backlash. Coalition support fell 2.8 points in three months, trailing opposition by 10.7 points. Electoral defeat likely in September, with SD poised for cabinet entry or Social Democrat return.

Orpo's coalition with the far-right Finns Party has collapsed from majority to 35.5%, losing parliamentary viability. Opposition filed confidence motion after unemployment rose 100k. May 15 drone incident closed Helsinki airport amid security tensions. Finns Party holds Finance/Interior portfolios, mainstreaming anti-immigrant agenda despite own polling decline. Coalition down 7+ points since formation.

Mitsotakis has fallen from a 40.6% landslide in 2023 to ~30% now, losing his parliamentary majority projection. The OPEKEPE farming subsidy scandal in May 2025 forced ministerial resignations and damaged his standing. Greek Solution (far-right) has grown to 8.6%. With elections due in 2027, Mitsotakis is attempting recovery through debt reduction messaging and Middle East diplomacy, but faces serious governability questions and a fragmented opposition.

Stocker's anti-FPÖ coalition, formed after the far-right won the 2024 election, is collapsing in polls. The coalition has dropped 4.5 points in three months to 46.5% while FPÖ surges to 36.4%. Despite January's economic recovery package targeting growth and inflation, Austria's post-recession struggles fuel far-right momentum. The government's exclusion of the election-winning FPÖ appears unsustainable—snap elections would likely hand Kickl decisive victory.

De Wever's Arizona coalition retains a comfortable 57% seat majority 15 months in, with the PM enjoying record-high personal popularity. However, the far-right Vlaams Belang holds 16% despite the cordon sanitaire. Recent controversies include De Wever's Russia negotiation comments and a diplomatic clash with the US ambassador over antisemitism accusations regarding circumciser prosecutions, yet coalition cohesion remains solid.

Luxon's National leads a fragile coalition with just 62 seats, but NZ First has surged from 6.1% at election to 11.7%, giving Peters kingmaker leverage. Recent crisis: Peters publicly released emails undermining Luxon's Iran war stance, forcing crisis talks. National dropped to 30% in polls, triggering failed leadership challenge in April. Coalition holds narrow lead but centrist weakness and populist partner's growing power signal late Phase I stress six months before election.

Meloni's far-right coalition has eroded from 46% to 43% over three months and would lose its parliamentary majority. Internal tensions surfaced this week as coalition partners clashed over electoral reform and Culture Ministry dismissals revealed fissures after March's referendum defeat. Despite this, Meloni remains Italy's dominant political figure, governing pragmatically while maintaining EU commitments rather than pursuing wholesale institutional destruction.

Babiš's ANO-SPD-Motorists coalition polls at 45.7%, down from 53.6% at election and sliding since February. On May 12, he ended Czech payments for Ukraine's shell pipeline while keeping it running, and scrapped annual euro readiness reports. Mass protests in March drew 200,000. SPD Speaker Okamura removed Ukraine's flag from parliament. Far-right in government, eroding support, institutional stress rising.

Trump's approval has cratered to 37.5% after diplomatic humiliation in China where Xi called America a declining nation, alongside a stalled Iran war ceasefire. High gas prices and voter frustration drive 48% strong disapproval. Democrats lead generic ballot by 5+ points with midterm wave building. Peak destruction phase of far-right governance.

Magyar won a historic supermajority one week ago, ending Orbán's 16-year authoritarian rule. Polling shows his support rising to 66% post-election. He's moving aggressively on institutional reform—demanding Orbán-appointed officials resign by May 31, reinstating the EU flag in parliament, and pledging corruption investigations. Early honeymoon but faces embedded Fidesz networks and Orbán's proven comeback ability.

Fico's government is in active institutional destruction phase, having captured public broadcasting, dismantled anti-corruption offices, and rewritten criminal codes. His May 9 Moscow visit and the EU Parliament's April 29 vote to freeze funds (418-207) mark peak confrontation with Brussels. Coalition polling has collapsed from 30% to 28.7% as opposition PS leads at 19.3%, signaling probable 2027 defeat. Far-right Republika rises to 10.9% outside coalition.

Milei's approval collapsed from 41.4% in February to 35% by April amid corruption scandals involving Chief of Staff Adorni and mass protests over university funding cuts on May 12. Real wages fell 9% below pre-election levels as his austerity chainsaw dismantles public services. Despite institutional damage and rising opposition, his October 2025 midterm victory and Trump's backing sustain his radical libertarian agenda.

Netanyahu's far-right coalition faces imminent collapse after United Torah Judaism's spiritual leader called for Knesset dissolution on May 12 over failed Haredi military exemption legislation. Coalition dropped from 53 to 49-50 seats since February, with 42% of 2022 Likud voters considering defection. This represents late-stage Phase III destruction—institutional damage from judicial reform, October 7 failures, and far-right governance—approaching electoral defeat.

Erdoğan's approval rebounded to 46% in March 2026 after hitting 38% earlier, but he governs through intensifying repression. His March 2026 arrest of Istanbul Mayor İmamoğlu—his main 2028 challenger—epitomizes authoritarian consolidation. Despite 58% inflation and losing his parliamentary majority, Erdoğan pursues institutional capture over reform, arresting hundreds of opposition figures while attempting to lure foreign investment with tax breaks. The PKK's May dissolution offers a rare positive.

Tusk's coalition has fractured after Poland 2050 split in February, losing parliamentary majority. Combined far-right (Confederation plus KKP) now polls at 24%, up from 20% three months ago. Presidential vetoes by PiS-backed Nawrocki block reforms. This week Poland signed EU defense loan bypassing presidential obstruction, but coalition tensions persist with election due 2027.

Carney's Liberals maintain a commanding 10-point lead with 56% approval, having converted their minority to a majority via byelections. The PPC far-right remains marginalized at 1%. However, majorities say he's fallen short on housing affordability and cost of living—the root causes that could fuel future dissatisfaction. Classic Phase IV reality check: high approval meets unfulfilled promises on pocketbook issues.

Lee came to power after predecessor Yoon's martial law crisis and impeachment. Nearly one year in, approval has softened from 67% peak to 59.7% amid livelihood cost concerns, though KOSPI hit 7,500 and current account reached records. Democratic Party chair Jung's May 3 campaign missteps distracted from economic wins. Lee governs with unified control, delivering on competence-driven agenda, but faces typical first-year reality checks in Phase IV.

Sheinbaum's continuation government faces mounting pressure in her second year. US indictment of Sinaloa Governor Rocha Moya on cartel ties has created a sovereignty crisis, with Sheinbaum demanding evidence before extradition. Reports of alleged CIA operations on Mexican soil further strained relations. Despite tensions culminating in a May 15 call with Trump, her Morena supermajority remains intact and she's arresting corrupt officials within her own party—unlike predecessors.

D66 leader Jetten took office February 2026 after defeating the Wilders-backed coalition in a snap election. Four-party center coalition holds for now, with PVV the largest opposition force. Early Phase IV honeymoon: mandate fresh, government cohesion intact, public optimism for change. The doom-loop question is whether the underlying causes that got Wilders elected get addressed this time.
